ogTitle: Gunns Plains vs Hadspen: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gunns Plains and Hadspen.
heading: Gunns Plains vs Hadspen — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gunns Plains, Tasmania and Hadspen, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gunns Plains has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Hadspen (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gunns Plains or Hadspen safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gunns Plains has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Hadspen scores 60/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gunns Plains scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Hadspen scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gunns Plains or Hadspen?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gunns Plains has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
